response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years across web, backend, and some systems programming, but his profile is a poor match for this role. His C++ exposure is minimal and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project), while the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise. He shows no evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practice, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or automation-industry experience. His low-latency DLang work demonstrates relevant systems and concurrency thinking, but it does not offset the core C++ and industrial-cybersecurity gaps.
strengths: Strong systems/performance background (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, high-throughput micro-services)
Broad full-stack versatility with Node.js, TypeScript, and React matching the 'advantageous' web skills
Solid engineering-practice culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Very limited and outdated professional C++ experience for a C++-centric senior role
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory is web/product-focused, suggesting a misalignment with embedded/industrial platform work
